title: Place value when multiplying and dividing by 10
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
topic_path: Place value when multiplying and dividing by 10

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank so the sentence accurately explains the relationship between $430{,}100$ and $43{,}010$.**


$430{,}100$ is [[☃ dropdown 1]] $43{,}010$.
hints:
Each digit of $430{,}100$ is one place to the left of the same digit in the number $43{,}010$.

Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-: |:-:
$$ | $\greenD4$ | $\blueD3$  | $\purpleC0$ | $\maroonD1$  | $\tealD0$
$\greenD4$ | $\blueD3$ | $\purpleC0$  | $\maroonD1$  | $\tealD0$  | $\goldD0$

Moving each digit to the left means multiplying by $10$: 
  
$43{,}010\times 10=430{,}100$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank so the sentence accurately explains the relationship between $650$ and $65$.**

$650$ is [[☃ dropdown 1]] $65$.
hints:
Each digit of ${650}$ is one place to the left of the same digit in the number ${65}$.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$$ | $\greenD6$ | $\blueD5$
$\greenD6$ | $\blueD5$ | $\purpleC0$

Moving each digit to the left means multiplying by $10$: 
  
${65}\times 10={650}$
$650$ is $10$ times as much as $65$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank so the sentence accurately explains the relationship between $8{,}700$ and $870$.**

$8{,}700$ is [[☃ dropdown 2]] $870$.
hints:
Each digit of ${8{,}700}$ is one place to the left of the same digit in the number ${870}$.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$$ | $\greenD8$ | $\blueD7$  | $\purpleC0$ 
$\greenD8$ | $\blueD7$ | $\purpleC0$  | $\maroonD0$

Moving each digit to the left means multiplying by $10$: 
  
${870}\times 10={8{,}700}$
$8{,}700$ is $10$ times as much as $870$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank so the sentence accurately explains the relationship between $90$ and $9$.**

$90$ is [[☃ dropdown 1]] $9$.
hints:
Each digit of ${90}$ is one place to the left of the same digit in the number ${9}$.

 Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$$ | $\greenD9$ 
$\greenD9$ | $\blueD0$

Moving each digit to the left means multiplying by $10$: 
  
${9}\times 10={90}$
$90$ is $10$ times as much as $9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank so the sentence accurately explains the relationship between $22{,}000$ and $2{,}200$.**

$22{,}000$ is [[☃ dropdown 1]] $2{,}200$.
hints:
Each digit of ${22{,}000}$ is one place to the left of the same digit in the number ${2{,}200}$.

Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-: |:-:
$$ | $\greenD2$ | $\blueD2$  | $\purpleC0$ | $\maroonD0$
$\greenD2$ | $\blueD2$ | $\purpleC0$  | $\maroonD0$  | $\tealD0$

Moving each digit to the left means multiplying by $10$: 
  
${2{,}200}\times 10={22{,}000}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Multiply.

$721\times 10$  

**What will be the place value of the $7$ after we multiply?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueD7$ | $2$ | $1$

The $\blueD7$ in $\blueD721$ is in  the $\blueD{\text{hundreds}}$ place.

Now, let's multiply $\blueD721\times10$. 


Multiplying by $10$ moves every digit one place to the left.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$$ |$\leftarrow\blueD{\fbox7}$ | $\fbox2$ | $\fbox1$

$\blueD721\times 10=\blueD7{,}210$

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
 $\blueD7$ | $2$ | $1$ | $0$

The $\blueD7$ moves to the $\blueD{\text{thousands}}$ place.

assessment_items.question_content:
Multiply.

$5{,}600\times 10$  

**What will be the place value of the $5$ after we multiply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$\blueD5$ | $6$ | $0$ | $0$

The $\blueD5$ in $\blueD5{,}600$ is in  the $\blueD{\text{thousands}}$ place.

Now, let's multiply $\blueD5{,}600\times10$. 


Multiplying by $10$ moves every digit one place to the left.

Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
|$\leftarrow\blueD{\fbox5}$ | $\fbox6$ |${\fbox0}$ | $\fbox0$ 

$\blueD5{,}600\times 10=\blueD56{,}000$

Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$\blueD5$ | $6$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The $\blueD5$ moves to the $\blueD{\text{ten thousands}}$ place.

assessment_items.question_content:
Multiply.

$49\times 10$  

**What will be the place value of the $9$ after we multiply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $4$ | $\blueD9$

The $\blueD9$ in $4\blueD9$ is in  the $\blueD{\text{ones}}$ place.

Now, let's multiply $4\blueD9\times10$. 

Multiplying by $10$ moves every digit one place to the left.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\leftarrow\fbox4$ | $\leftarrow\blueD{\fbox9}$

$4\blueD9\times 10=4\blueD90$

Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
 $4$ | $\blueD9$ | $0$

The $\blueD9$ moves to the $\blueD{\text{tens}}$ place.

assessment_items.question_content:
Divide.

$37{,}140\div10$

**What will be the place value of the $1$ after we divide?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$3$ | $7$ | $\blueD1$ | $4$ | $0$

The $\blueD1$ in $37{,}\blueD140$ is in  the $\blueD{\text{hundreds}}$ place.

Now, let's divide $37{,}\blueD140\div10$. 


Dividing by $10$ moves every digit one place to the right.

Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
|$\rightarrow\fbox3$ | $\fbox7$ |$\blueD{\fbox1}$ | $\fbox4$ | $\fbox0$

$37{,}\blueD140\div 10=37\blueD14$

Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$$ | $$ | $3$ | $7$ | $\blueD1$ | $4$

The $\blueD1$ moves to the $\blueD{\text{tens}}$ place.

assessment_items.question_content:
Divide.

$2{,}380\div10$

**What will be the place value of the $8$ after we divide?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$2$ | $3$ | $\blueD8$ | $0$

The $\blueD8$ in $2{,}3\blueD80$ is in  the $\blueD{\text{tens}}$ place.

Now, let's divide $2{,}3\blueD80\div10$. 


Dividing by $10$ moves every digit one place to the right.

Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
|$\rightarrow\fbox2$ | $\fbox3$ |$\blueD{\fbox8}$ | $\fbox0$ 

$2{,}3\blueD80\div 10=23\blueD8$

Ten thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:
$$ | $$ | $2$ | $3$ | $\blueD8$

The $\blueD8$ moves to the $\blueD{\text{ones}}$ place.
If we have $54$ ones and we multiply them by $10$, then each of the ones becomes a ten.
Now, instead of having $54$ ones, we have $54$ tens.
$54 \text{ ones} \times 10=\ 54\text{ tens}$
If we have $93$ tens and we multiply them by $10$, then each of the tens becomes a hundred.
Now, instead of having $93$ tens, we have $93$ hundreds.
$93 \text{ tens} \times 10=\ 93\text{ hundreds}$
If we have $7$ hundreds and we multiply them by $10$, then each of the hundreds becomes a thousand.
Now, instead of having $7$ hundreds, we have $7$ thousands.
$7 \text{ hundreds} \times 10=\ 7\text{ thousands}$
If we have $18$ thousands and we multiply them by $10$, then each of the thousands becomes a ten thousand.
Now, instead of having $18$ thousands, we have $18$ ten thousands.
$18 \text{ thousands} \times 10=\ 18\text{ ten thousands}$
If we have $212$ tens and we divide them by $10$, then each of the tens becomes a one.
Now, instead of having $212$ tens, we have $212$ ones.
$212 \text { tens} \div 10= 212 \text { ones}$
If we have $66$ hundreds and we divide them by $10$, then each of the hundreds becomes a ten.
Now, instead of having $66$ hundreds, we have $66$ tens.
$66 \text { hundreds} \div 10= 66 \text { tens}$
If we have $8$ thousands and we divide them by $10$, then each of the thousands becomes a hundred.
Now, instead of having $8$ thousands, we have $8$ hundreds.
$8 \text { thousands} \div 10= 8 \text { hundreds}$
If we have $75$ tens and we divide them by $10$, then each of the tens becomes a one.
Now, instead of having $75$ tens, we have $75$ ones.
$75 \text { tens} \div 10= 75 \text { ones}$
